channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Gunzilla CEO Fires Back At Claims Of Not Paying Employees
item.content:encoded:
The CEO of Gunzilla Games is firing back against recent claims that the company hasn’t paid employee salaries for months.

Earlier this week, talent acquisition lead Anna Savina posted on LinkedIn, claiming that Gunzilla Games failed to pay a “significant outstanding debt that covers several months.”

After her post, other employees replied, suggesting they were dealing with similar situations. That led Gunzilla Games CEO Vlad Korolev to post a lengthy message on Twitter regarding the claims.

Right away, Korolev called the claims nothing more than “FUD”, which stands for fear, uncertainty, and doubt, and that it was only done to “farm views”. He said that it was a way to target “the biggest web3 game ever created.”

He then went on to focus on the studio’s NFT battle royale game Off The Grid. Korolev said that if people want to spread “FUD”, he’d be willing to set up a live dashboard to see new players joining in real time.

After more talk of the game’s growth, Korolev finally went back to the claims levied against him and Gunzilla Games.

“Today there is a new narrative from haters — that Gunzilla incorrectly laid off contractors or paid them with delays,” he said. “Yes, we are optimizing costs — like every company in gaming, crypto, and tech is doing right now.

“We have been doing this for over a year. And yes, to not disrupt company operations, some payments may be scheduled in a way that works for the company’s cash flow — not always for everyone individually. That’s the reality of the world we live in.”

Korolev continued by adding that the company works at a pace “that ensures the company continues moving forward”, and that they “honor every obligation.”

While he says they “apologize for any inconvenience this has caused”, Korolev says that “one of the loudest voices” against the company, who he says was a contractor who finished their work a week ago, was “repaid immediately”. That said, he didn’t address the other claims against it from long-time employees.

Instead, he doubled down on those against the studio and their practices, which includes there not being “a single day where anyone worked in a “work-life balance” mode”, haters.

“And while our dear haters keep trying to find new reasons why Gunzilla should fail — we will keep responding with achievements,” he said.

For what it’s worth, Savina provided an update after she took her claims public that her specific situation has been addressed by the company. There hasn’t been an update on the other employees who made similar accusations.

What do you think of the situation unfolding at Gunzilla Games? Leave your thoughts in the comments below, and in this official Insider Gaming Discord.

In other news, Amazon Luna is making major changes to what it offers that will impact every user of the service. And for even more Insider Gaming delivered directly to your inbox, sign up for our newsletter.

Mike has been covering the gaming industry since 2012, and has reported on some of the largest events in the industry while also working as an investigative reporter. Outside of…

More from Mike Straw

item.title: Amazon Luna Makes Major Changes That Impact All Users
item.content:encoded:
Amazon is making major changes to Luna that are going to impact all users, especially those who utilize third-party purchases and subscriptions.

Starting April 10, Amazon Luna is no longer offering game stores, individual game purchases, or third-party subscriptions. The company says it’s being done based on feedback from players.

“At Amazon, we’re always looking for ways to better serve our players,” the company said. “Your feedback has been clear: you want easy access to great games, more social experiences, and a steady flow of new content from developers you know and love.”

“As our library continues to grow, more of that content is available to Prime members-and that’s where we’re focusing our future.”

Looking at the specific changes, Amazon is eliminating the following:

  • A-la-carte game purchases
  • Bring Your Own Library is no longer supported
  • Ubisoft+ and Jackbox Games subscriptions have been discontinued
  • Third-party game stores such as Ubisoft, GOG, and EA are being removed from Luna.

Even The Games You Own Are Leaving Amazon Luna

It’s not just about these features being removed from the Luna service. Even games that have been purchased are being removed from the platform.

After June 10, 2026, any game you may have purchased through a third-party store via Luna will no longer be playable. You’ll have to go through the proper third-party platform to continue to play.

If you have an Ubisoft+ or Jackbox Games subscription purchased through Luna, it will renew one final time before being automatically cancelled. If you want to keep it, you have to go through their channels to sign back up.

For those who’ve been utilizing the Bring Your Own Library feature, that will be removed on June 3.

Finally, if you have save data you want to download and bring over to where your games will be, you can do that. However, that will only be available for a 90-day period after June 10.

What do you think of the changes Amazon is making to its Luna gaming service? Leave your thoughts in the comments below, and in this official Insider Gaming Discord.

In other news, read about how Backyard Sports was brought back to life by a former elementary school teacher. And for even more Insider Gaming delivered directly to your inbox, sign up for our newsletter.

Mike has been covering the gaming industry since 2012, and has reported on some of the largest events in the industry while also working as an investigative reporter. Outside of…

item.title: When Does Battlefield 6 Season 3 Release? Battlefield 6 Season 2 End Date & Times, Explained
item.content:encoded:
Battlefield 6 Season 3 will be here before you know it to dethrone Battlefield 6 Season 2. The player count might not be as dominant as it once was, but tens of thousands of players still log in daily. If you’re one of them, read on for BF 6 Season 3 details.

Battlefield 6 Season 2 Nightfall was a welcome addition after its delay. Battlefield Studios continues to stagger content drops over the course of a few weeks, and Hunter / Prey will be the final one of Season 2.

Get ready for Season 3.

When Does Battlefield 6 Season 2 End?

Battlefield 6 Season 2 is expected to end on May 12, as it coincides with the end of the Battlefield 6 Season 2 Battle Pass countdown.

This lines up with previous BF 6 content and matches up with the four-week strategy EA uses for each mini-mid-season update. Once Season 2’s Hunter / Prey is live, then the final four-week deadline is triggered.

  • More Insider Gaming: Battlefield 6 Devs Used Multiple AI Tools During Development

Leaks are already starting to come out about Season 3, including weapons and gear still lingering in the game files. So let’s see when it’s dropping.

When is Battlefield 6 Season 3 Coming Out? Release Date & Times

Battlefield 6 doesn’t have too much downtime in between seasons, so expect Battlefield 6 Season 3 to commence on May 12, the same day Season 2 expires, at 4 AM PT.

According to reliable Battlefield dataminer, temporyal, Plaza, Isolated, and Golmud Railway are maps that are still likely to drop.

  • More Insider Gaming: Pro CoD Players Say Battlefield 6 is a ‘Worse COD’ For Dad Gamers

Furthermore, some weapons have been discovered:

  • L115A3
  • RPK-74M
  • M16A3
  • PP-19 Vityaz

Should Battlefield 6 Season 3 begin at the same time as other seasons, here’s a look at worldwide launch times.

What do you think about the state of Battlefield 6 right now? Has EA fumbled? What does Battlefield 6 need? Share your thoughts and feedback via the Insider Gaming Discord.

If you missed it, read about Battlefield Hardline’s future on PS4 and Xbox One.

Written by

Evergreen Editor

Andy is Insider Gaming's Senior Evergreen Editor and has been in the games journalism sphere in one way or another for over 7 years. His video game taste is as…

More from Andrew Highton

item.title: Marvel’s Guardians of the Galaxy Seemingly Coming to Switch 2
item.content:encoded:
A new rating for Marvel’s Guardians of the Galaxy has been spotted, hinting at an imminent Switch 2 release.

Nintendo has made it clear with the latest Switch 2 console that third-party games are now a thing. We have already seen titles like Cyberpunk 2077, Resident Evil Requiem, and many more released on the platform. Now, it seems like Nintendo is moving towards bringing Marvel into the fray as well.

Marvel’s Guardians of the Galaxy Rated for Switch 2 in Taiwan

Spotted by Gematsu, the Taiwan Digital Game Rating Committee has rated Marvel’s Guardians of the Galaxy for the Switch 2. The platform clearly mentions Switch 2, the rating is PG-12, and the company is listed as Koch Media GmbH. However, the launch date section says “not approved”, so we don’t know when it could be released for the platform.

For those unaware, Marvel’s Guardians of the Galaxy is a story-focused, single-player third-person action-adventure developed by Eidos-Montréal. The title has you playing as the titular Star-Lord in an original story where the characters are caught up in another struggle over the fate of the universe.

You play as Star-Lord, but you can call any of the Guardians to your aid and have them use their signature attacks on the enemies. Having played the game myself, I can say this game has some of the best writing and adrenaline-fueled action sequences that a Marvel fan will thoroughly enjoy.

In other news, here’s more about the new Marvel Rivals update, which adds the Lower Manhattan map. Additionally, new leaks indicate that another iconic Marvel character is set to appear in Fortnite. What are your thoughts on Marvel’s Guardians of the Galaxy coming to the Switch 2? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Professor Layton and the New World of Steam devs confirming a PS5 and Stream release. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.
